suggestions.text: Rest today and keep the load light — recovery matters just as much as effort.
Your body may be signalling that it needs a slower day. Prioritise sleep and low-stimulation activities.
A consistent wind-down routine tonight could help reset your sleep quality for the rest of the week.
A short low-intensity walk should help reset your momentum without adding stress.
Even 10 minutes of movement today can shift your energy and mood in a positive direction.
Getting back to light activity is the fastest way to rebuild the exercise habit after a gap.
Consider reaching out to someone you trust for support — connection is one of the fastest mood regulators.
Even a brief check-in with a friend or family member can provide meaningful relief on hard days.
This pattern is high-risk. A clinician check-in is the safest next step — please reach out today.
Given what your logs are showing, speaking with a healthcare professional this week is strongly recommended.
Add one more log today so the system can tailor guidance more accurately to your current state.
Consistent daily check-ins unlock more personalised insights. Try logging at the same time each day.
Keep your routine steady and keep logging — stability is its own form of progress.
Anchor your day with one small predictable habit — routine is a powerful mood stabiliser.
Protecting your sleep window tonight is the highest-leverage action you can take for tomorrow's energy.
Your recent sleep pattern suggests some disruption — try a fixed bedtime for the next 3 nights.
A 3-minute breathing reset before your next task can help reduce the stress load you're carrying.
Taking a moment to notice what you're feeling — without judgement — is a small but powerful act.
